title: The One with the Invitation

jokes.text: If I died, people would know I was here only by the ass print on this chair!
jokes.setup: Friends feeling like they're not accomplishing anything meaningful
jokes.punchline: The only legacy would be a butt impression on furniture
jokes.explanation: Strong visual metaphor for lack of achievement, perfectly delivered with Joey's earnestness. Great economy and quotable phrasing.

jokes.text: Not something stupid. Something huge!
jokes.setup: Monica suggests climbing Mt. Everest as something big to do
jokes.punchline: Chandler dismisses climbing the world's tallest mountain as 'stupid'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm, though the irony is somewhat obvious. Good character moment.

jokes.text: It's just steep!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Mt. Everest's difficulty
jokes.punchline: Joey reduces the world's most dangerous climb to just being 'steep'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - massively underestimating complexity. Great economy in delivery.

jokes.text: It'd be nice to leave an ass print on Everest
jokes.setup: Continuing the Everest discussion
jokes.punchline: Monica references Joey's earlier ass print comment in new context
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that escalates the absurdity by applying it to Everest.

jokes.text: And you would die.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions you could die climbing Everest
jokes.punchline: Ross matter-of-factly confirms they specifically would die
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan escalation, though somewhat predictable. Ross's scientific certainty adds character flavor.

jokes.text: While we're at the video store, we could rent Die Hard!
jokes.setup: Discussion of getting an Everest video as compromise
jokes.punchline: Joey's priorities immediately shift to action movies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - easily distracted by movies. Character-specific but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: But one of these days we'll get off our butts and rent Die Hard again!
jokes.setup: The grand adventure plans have devolved completely
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically presents renting a movie as their ultimate achievement
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that bookends the scene, highlighting how their 'huge' plans became pathetically small.

jokes.text: We'll say that Ben addressed them
jokes.setup: Emily asks if the invitations are neat enough
jokes.punchline: Ross suggests blaming messy handwriting on his young son
jokes.explanation: Mild Ross humor about deflecting responsibility to his child. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I wouldn't be comfortable having any of my old lovers there
jokes.setup: Discussion about inviting Rachel to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Emily's passive-aggressive way of expressing discomfort
jokes.explanation: Sets up Emily's controlling nature. More character development than comedy gold.

jokes.text: And I just want a million dollars!
jokes.setup: Ross expressing his desire to be married again
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically equates his romantic wish with an impossible financial one
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic retort format. Gets the job done but lacks originality.

jokes.text: I knew. / You did? / I figured you thought I was Monica's geeky brother. / I did.
jokes.setup: Ross admits he knew Rachel had a crush on him in high school
jokes.punchline: Rachel confirms she also thought he was a geek
jokes.explanation: Perfect mutual deflation moment. Great economy in the back-and-forth structure, very relatable awkwardness.

jokes.text: Try not to let my intense vulnerability become a factor
jokes.setup: Ross about to ask Rachel out
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of his desperate position
jokes.explanation: Smart self-aware humor that acknowledges Ross's awkwardness while being genuinely funny. Good quotable phrase.

jokes.text: It's never gonna happen
jokes.setup: Phoebe has been predicting big passion in Ross's future with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Immediate deadpan contradiction of her own prediction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - mystical predictions immediately undercut by harsh reality. Great timing and economy.

jokes.text: Actually, it's not so much a question. It's more of a general wondering-ment
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she has a question
jokes.punchline: Making up the word 'wonderingment' while overthinking
jokes.explanation: Cute made-up word that shows Rachel's tendency to overthink. Mildly charming but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Listen, missy! If you want this cart, you'll have to take me with it!
jokes.setup: Rachel learning to do laundry and being protective of her cart
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic defense of a laundry cart
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment showing Rachel's newfound assertiveness in mundane situations.

jokes.text: I don't need your stupid ship!
jokes.setup: Ross says 'this ship has sailed' about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Rachel takes the metaphor literally in anger
jokes.explanation: Clever literalization of a common metaphor during an emotional moment. Good quotable line.

jokes.text: A break from us
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests getting frozen yogurt to cool off
jokes.punchline: Ross clarifies he meant a relationship break, not a snack break
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that creates a pivotal show moment. The miscommunication drives major plot points.

jokes.text: Don't answer! / You wanted to talk about it. How was she?
jokes.setup: Rachel asks about Ross's encounter with another woman
jokes.punchline: Immediately tells him not to answer, then asks again anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect capture of conflicted emotions - wanting and not wanting to know simultaneously.

jokes.text: Once a cheater, always a cheater
jokes.setup: Emily talking about her mother's opinion of Ross
jokes.punchline: Ironic given Emily will later have an affair
jokes.explanation: Works better on rewatch when the irony becomes apparent. Functional in the moment.

jokes.text: WE WERE ON A BREAK!
jokes.setup: The ongoing debate about Ross's actions during their 'break'
jokes.punchline: Ross's passionate, repeated defense that becomes his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase that became a cultural reference. Perfect character moment that encapsulates Ross's defensive nature and the relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: You're sitting here in a hallway with a 28-year-old cheerleader with a fat lip
jokes.setup: Joey giving Chandler advice about Emily
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurdity of Chandler's current situation
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment with specific absurd details. Joey being unexpectedly wise.

jokes.text: Handkerchief? No, I got my invitation
jokes.setup: Monica suggests bringing tissue paper to cry at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Joey plans to use the wedding invitation as a tissue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - practical but completely inappropriate. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: So I didn't know that. But you should see your faces
jokes.setup: Phoebe claims she knew about flying restrictions in third trimester
jokes.punchline: Admits she didn't know but enjoyed their reactions
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe misdirection - admits to lying just to see their reactions. Meta-humor about the audience.

jokes.text: Because I already bought my ticket
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether to boycott if Rachel doesn't come
jokes.punchline: Chandler's selfish but honest concern about money
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler moment - practical concerns over emotional solidarity. Character-appropriate selfishness.

jokes.text: Remember when he fell in love with Carol and got her that expensive crystal duck?
jokes.setup: Rachel receiving an expensive gift from Ross
jokes.punchline: Monica points out Ross's pattern of expensive gifts when in love
jokes.explanation: Good character insight that establishes Ross's romantic patterns. Sets up Rachel's realization.

jokes.text: Isn't that just kick-you-in-the-crotch, spit-on-your-neck fantastic?
jokes.setup: Ross excited about reuniting with Julie
jokes.punchline: Rachel's over-the-top sarcastic enthusiasm with violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly excessive sarcasm with memorable violent imagery. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: No, you hang up. You! / You didn't hang up! / She didn't hang up either
jokes.setup: Ross and Julie doing the typical 'you hang up' routine
jokes.punchline: Neither actually hangs up, creating awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Familiar relationship cliche played for awkwardness. Functional but not original.

jokes.text: and your cat
jokes.setup: Rachel calling to say she's happy for Ross
jokes.punchline: Accidentally refers to Julie as his cat in her nervousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect nervous mistake that reveals Rachel's scattered emotional state. Great economy and character insight.

jokes.text: When were you under me?
jokes.setup: Rachel says she's 'over' Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross points out the logical flaw in the phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever literalization of common phrase with slight sexual innuendo. Good Ross pedantic moment.

jokes.text: No, you just rolled over the juice box
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Ross and Emily being intimate
jokes.punchline: The mood is killed by a child's juice box interruption
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy deflation. Works but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: And hey! Just so you know, it's not that common. It doesn't happen to every guy, and it is a big deal.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel's failed romantic encounter
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive explanation about erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross defensiveness about an embarrassing topic. Very quotable and character-specific cringe humor.

jokes.text: So how many cameras are actually on you?
jokes.setup: Monica says the camera adds 10 pounds
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies she looks like multiple cameras are on her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mathematical joke with perfect setup and devastating delivery. Classic Chandler wit that became widely quoted.

jokes.text: Looking good, Mr. Kotter!
jokes.setup: Ross in his tuxedo for prom
jokes.punchline: Dad references Welcome Back Kotter TV show
jokes.explanation: Basic pop culture reference humor. Dates the show but fits the dad character.

jokes.text: See? He's her lobster!
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel finally getting together after the prom video
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unique metaphor for soulmates
jokes.explanation: Iconic Phoebe moment that created a lasting cultural reference. Perfect blend of absurdist humor and genuine sentiment. Became synonymous with the show.

jokes.text: And your license expired
jokes.setup: Phoebe points out Rachel doesn't have a car to drive her to hospital
jokes.punchline: Rachel adds that her license is also expired
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of incompetence. Functional but not exceptional comedy.

jokes.text: It's better! You can't go to a museum in your underwear. / You could, but just the one time.
jokes.setup: Chandler says watching museum videos is better than going
jokes.punchline: Joey acknowledges the underwear limitation with experience implied
jokes.explanation: Good two-part joke with Joey's characteristic implication of personal experience with inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: I bet the British version is good!
jokes.setup: Discussion about renting Die Hard in London
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks there's a British version of the American movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic about movies and international versions. Good callback that bookends the episode theme.

tmdb_overview: Ross and Emily send wedding invitations. Ross and Rachel reflect on their relationship. Joey and Chandler search for life purpose.
